figures.caption: TABLE II KNOWLEDGE-GRAPH STATISTICS FOR EACH BENCHMARK.
TABLE I THE PROPOSED METHOD AMONG REPRESENTATIVE GRAPH RAG SYSTEMS.

Fig. 1. Two-phase architecture. The indexing phase (top) extracts entities and relations from the corpus via an LLM, resolves and embeds them, and stores the result as a typed knowledge graph in Neo4j. The query phase (bottom) uses an LLM to map the question to seed nodes, runs spreading activation for T iterations in a single Cypher query, and passes the activated subgraph to an LLM to generate the answer.

Fig. 2. Knowledge-graph schema. Document and paragraph nodes preserve the original structure of the corpus; :MENTIONS edges record entity mentions at two levels: the document (aggregated) and the paragraph (with exact location). Entities and their typed binary relations form the basis for multi-hop reasoning. The self-loop denotes the :RELATES_TO edge, which connects two entities and stores the relation label as a property.
TABLE V COMPONENT ABLATION OVER ELEVEN CONFIGURATIONS (n = 500).

Fig. 3. Sensitivity analysis over the propagation depth (left) and the decay factor (right) at n = 500 per dataset. Stars mark the default configuration T = 3, α = 0.7. The dashed horizontal lines show the naive-vector baseline F1 on each dataset.
